span.p: NO — or need a universal fallback

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;
display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Is this for high-assurance / regulated / privileged access? — admin, finance move, regulated PII, NIST AAL3

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Can you mandate hardware? — issued security keys or platform authenticators with non-exportable keys

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Is the audience able to use device biometrics / a platform authenticator?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Hardware-backed FIDO2/WebAuthn — security key or device-bound passkey, non-exportable. AAL3-eligible, phishing-resistant.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Device-bound passkey where available + a phishing-resistant fallback; document the AAL3 gap. NEVER drop to SMS for AAL3.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Passkey / WebAuthn as the offered second factor — phishing-resistant, AAL2-strong. Supabase Passkeys = beta, gate + keep fallback.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: TOTP authenticator app — Supabase TOTP MFA, free/default. Phishing-resistant? NO, but far stronger than SMS.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Considering SMS / phone OTP as a factor?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: AVOID as primary — SIM-swap + OTP-phishing-able. Use only as a last-resort recovery factor, never the AAL gate.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Proceed — offer 2+ factors so a lost device isn't a lockout
